plan:
- Design and fabricate a 12U CubeSat chassis using 7075-T6 aluminum and Ti-6Al-4V brackets, including CFRP optical bench and four spring-loaded solar array hinge assemblies, and a SAR antenna deployment hinge.
- Integrate a 200mm aperture TMA optical telescope with a back-side illuminated CMOS detector (Sony IMX455 or equivalent) and an X-band SAR system with a deployable 8x16 patch array antenna.
- Implement an on-board computer with a Xiphos Q8S or equivalent, an NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX (or Ubotica CogniSAT-XE1) AI accelerator, and 512 GB to 1 TB of rad-hard NAND flash storage.
- Develop an ADCS using two star trackers, a MEMS fiber optic gyro, six sun sensors, and two magnetometers, controlled by four reaction wheels and three magnetorquers.
- Configure an EPS with four deployable GaAs triple-junction solar panels (total ~0.45 m², ~80W BOL), a 100-150 Wh Li-ion battery pack, and a GomSpace NanoPower P60 (or DHV) PCDU with an unregulated 28V primary bus.
- Establish communication links with an X-band high-rate downlink (Syrlinks EWC27 or Tethers Unlimited SWIFT-XTS, 150 Mbps, 5W RF) and an S-band TT&C system (1W RF, 9.6 kbps uplink, 256 kbps downlink).

nodes.name: Main On-Board Computer
nodes.description: ARM Cortex-A9 dual-core + Xilinx Kintex-7 FPGA (XC7K160T), space-qualified, 4 GB DDR3 ECC RAM, 256 MB NOR flash, FreeRTOS/cFS. Handles SAR range compression, image pre-processing, data routing. Radiation tolerant ≥ 30 krad TID.

nodes.name: AI Accelerator Module
nodes.description: COTS (space-modified with shielding). 100 TOPS (INT8) performance for onboard AI inference. Shielded in 2mm aluminum vault + 0.5mm tantalum. Target ≤ 10 krad TID.
nodes.productName: NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX (16 GB) - Space Modified

nodes.name: Mass Storage Flash Module
nodes.description: 512 GB to 1 TB radiation-hardened NAND flash storage with hardware SECDED. FIFO ring buffer for raw imagery, priority queue for AI alerts. ≥ 100,000 write cycles.

nodes.name: Optical Focal Plane Array Detector
nodes.description: Back-side illuminated (BSI) CMOS, scientific grade. ≥ 9000 × 6000 pixels, 5.0–7.0 μm pixel pitch. PAN, Blue, Green, Red, NIR spectral channels. ≥ 30 fps burst, TDI mode.

nodes.name: SAR Transceiver Electronics
nodes.description: X-Band (9.6 GHz) radar system including GaN SSPA (20-40W peak RF), LNA (NF ≤ 2.5dB), DDS (AD9914 or equiv.), 12-bit ≥1 Gsps ADC, and TCXO (≤ 1 ppb stability). Handles LFM chirp waveform generation and raw data sampling. Onboard range compression.
nodes.productName: Custom X-band SAR Electronics (GaN SSPA, LNA, DDS, ADC)

nodes.name: Deployable SAR Patch Antenna
nodes.description: 500mm × 300mm deployed aperture, 8 × 16 (128 elements) patch array. ≥ 28 dBi gain. Electronic beam steering (±30° azimuth) via 4-bit phase shifters per element. RF connection via flexible coaxial harness.

nodes.name: Star Tracker (+Y Panel)
nodes.description: Primary star tracker for high-accuracy attitude knowledge. ≥ 4 Hz update rate, ≤ 5 arcsec (3σ) knowledge, ≥ 10° × 10° FOV, lost-in-space capable. Mounted on +Y panel.
nodes.productName: Blue Canyon Technologies NST or Sinclair Interplanetary ST-16RT2

nodes.name: Star Tracker (-Y Panel)
nodes.description: Redundant star tracker for high-accuracy attitude knowledge. ≥ 4 Hz update rate, ≤ 5 arcsec (3σ) knowledge, ≥ 10° × 10° FOV, lost-in-space capable. Mounted on -Y panel.
nodes.productName: Blue Canyon Technologies NST or Sinclair Interplanetary ST-16RT2

nodes.name: IMU / Gyroscope
nodes.description: High-frequency attitude data. ARW ≤ 0.003°/√hr, Bias stability ≤ 1°/hr. 100 Hz update rate.
nodes.productName: Analog Devices ADIS16480 (or MEMS FOG equivalent)

nodes.name: Coarse Sun Sensor (+X Face)
nodes.description: Used for initial acquisition and safe-mode attitude hold.

nodes.name: Coarse Sun Sensor (-X Face)
nodes.description: Used for initial acquisition and safe-mode attitude hold.

nodes.name: Coarse Sun Sensor (+Y Face)
nodes.description: Used for initial acquisition and safe-mode attitude hold.

nodes.name: Coarse Sun Sensor (-Y Face)
nodes.description: Used for initial acquisition and safe-mode attitude hold.

nodes.name: Coarse Sun Sensor (+Z Face)
nodes.description: Used for initial acquisition and safe-mode attitude hold.

nodes.name: Coarse Sun Sensor (-Z Face)
nodes.description: Used for initial acquisition and safe-mode attitude hold.

nodes.name: Fine Sun Sensor (+Z Face)
nodes.description: Provides precise solar tracking data, mounted on +Z face.

nodes.name: Magnetometer 1
nodes.description: Used for momentum desaturation scheduling with magnetorquers.

nodes.name: Magnetometer 2
nodes.description: Redundant magnetometer for momentum desaturation scheduling.

nodes.name: Reaction Wheel 1
nodes.description: Provides attitude control torque. ≥ 30 mNm·s angular momentum, ≥ 0.5 mNm torque. Part of pyramid configuration.

nodes.name: Reaction Wheel 2
nodes.description: Provides attitude control torque. ≥ 30 mNm·s angular momentum, ≥ 0.5 mNm torque. Part of pyramid configuration.

nodes.name: Reaction Wheel 3
nodes.description: Provides attitude control torque. ≥ 30 mNm·s angular momentum, ≥ 0.5 mNm torque. Part of pyramid configuration.

nodes.name: Reaction Wheel 4 (Redundant)
nodes.description: Redundant reaction wheel for attitude control. Skewed 54.7° from Z-axis. ≥ 30 mNm·s angular momentum, ≥ 0.5 mNm torque.

nodes.name: Magnetorquer X-axis
nodes.description: Used for desaturation of reaction wheels and safe-mode detumbling. Dipole moment ≥ 0.3 A·m².

nodes.name: Magnetorquer Y-axis
nodes.description: Used for desaturation of reaction wheels and safe-mode detumbling. Dipole moment ≥ 0.3 A·m².

nodes.name: Magnetorquer Z-axis
nodes.description: Used for desaturation of reaction wheels and safe-mode detumbling. Dipole moment ≥ 0.3 A·m².

nodes.name: ADCS Controller Board
nodes.description: Dedicated board for ADCS algorithms. Runs EKF at 100 Hz, PD control for fine pointing, and B-dot detumble algorithm. Interfaces with all ADCS sensors and actuators.

nodes.name: Power Conditioning & Distribution Unit
nodes.description: Manages power from solar arrays and battery. Unregulated 28V primary bus. Provides regulated 12V/5A, 5V/6A, 3.3V/4A rails. 28V pass-through rails (5A for SAR/X-Band, 3A for Jetson). Per-panel MPPT. Overcurrent protection (eFuse, latching relay).
nodes.productName: GomSpace NanoPower P60 or DHV Technology EPS-R

nodes.name: Li-ion Battery Pack
nodes.description: 100–150 Wh Li-ion battery pack, 7.4V (2S) or 14.8V (4S) nominal voltage. Operational SOC 20%-90%. Cell temperature monitoring.
nodes.productName: GomSpace BPX / Saft MP176065 / EaglePicher LCB

nodes.name: Deployable Solar Array Panel 1
nodes.description: GaAs triple-junction solar cells (XTJ-Prime or equivalent) with ≥ 30% efficiency. CFRP substrate, CMX coverglass. Contributes to ~80W BOL total power.

nodes.name: Deployable Solar Array Panel 2
nodes.description: GaAs triple-junction solar cells (XTJ-Prime or equivalent) with ≥ 30% efficiency. CFRP substrate, CMX coverglass. Contributes to ~80W BOL total power.

nodes.name: Deployable Solar Array Panel 3
nodes.description: GaAs triple-junction solar cells (XTJ-Prime or equivalent) with ≥ 30% efficiency. CFRP substrate, CMX coverglass. Contributes to ~80W BOL total power.

nodes.name: Deployable Solar Array Panel 4
nodes.description: GaAs triple-junction solar cells (XTJ-Prime or equivalent) with ≥ 30% efficiency. CFRP substrate, CMX coverglass. Contributes to ~80W BOL total power.

nodes.name: Fixed Body-Mounted Solar Panel 5
nodes.description: Body-mounted GaAs triple-junction solar cells. Contributes to ~80W BOL total power.

nodes.name: Fixed Body-Mounted Solar Panel 6
nodes.description: Body-mounted GaAs triple-junction solar cells. Contributes to ~80W BOL total power.

nodes.name: X-Band High-Rate Transmitter
nodes.description: 8.0–8.4 GHz, 150 Mbps (QPSK, rate 7/8 turbo code), 5 W RF output power. Downlinks imagery and AI alert packets. AES-256 encryption.
nodes.productName: Syrlinks EWC27 or Tethers Unlimited SWIFT-XTS

nodes.name: X-Band Phased Array Antenna
nodes.description: Fixed nadir-pointing 4-element patch phased array. ~10 dBi gain, ±25° HPBW. Connected to X-Band transmitter.

nodes.name: S-Band TT&C Transceiver Module
nodes.description: 2.025–2.110 GHz uplink, 2.200–2.290 GHz downlink. 9.6 kbps uplink, 256 kbps downlink. 1 W RF output. CCSDS TC/TM Space Packet Protocol. For telemetry, tracking & commanding.

nodes.name: S-Band Patch Antenna (+Z Face)
nodes.description: Provides full-sphere S-Band coverage, mounted on +Z face.

nodes.name: S-Band Patch Antenna (-Z Face)
nodes.description: Provides full-sphere S-Band coverage, mounted on -Z face.

nodes.name: Battery Heater Mat 1
nodes.description: 2W thermostatically controlled Kapton heater mat for battery pack. Activates if battery temperature falls below 5°C.

nodes.name: 12U CubeSat Chassis Structure
nodes.description: Primary structural frame for the 12U CubeSat (226x226x340mm), 6U upper payload bay, 6U lower bus bay. Made of Aluminum alloy 7075-T6. PC/104 and CDS Rev 14 compliant.

nodes.name: CFRP Optical Bench Plate
nodes.description: Carbon-fiber reinforced polymer optical bench plate for athermal telescope mounting. Isolates thermal distortion via bipod flexure mounts.

nodes.name: High-Stress Titanium Brackets
nodes.description: Grade 5 Titanium (Ti-6Al-4V) brackets for high-stress interfaces, e.g., securing payloads or ADCS components.

nodes.name: Solar Array Hinge Assembly 1
nodes.description: Spring-loaded hinge assembly for deploying solar panel 1. Designed to survive 14.1 g_rms random vibration.

nodes.name: Solar Array Hinge Assembly 2
nodes.description: Spring-loaded hinge assembly for deploying solar panel 2. Designed to survive 14.1 g_rms random vibration.

nodes.name: Solar Array Hinge Assembly 3
nodes.description: Spring-loaded hinge assembly for deploying solar panel 3. Designed to survive 14.1 g_rms random vibration.

nodes.name: Solar Array Hinge Assembly 4
nodes.description: Spring-loaded hinge assembly for deploying solar panel 4. Designed to survive 14.1 g_rms random vibration.

nodes.name: SAR Antenna Deployment Hinge
nodes.description: Torsion spring hinge for SAR antenna deployment, designed to be fail-safe (deployed configuration).

nodes.name: CubeSat Spring Ejector Interface
nodes.description: Standardized interface for deployment from common CubeSat ejectors.

nodes.name: Passive Radiator Panel (-Z Face)
nodes.description: Dedicated passive radiator panel on the -Z face for focal plane cooling. Black anodized surface for optimal radiation.

nodes.name: MLI Blankets (Telescope Barrel)
nodes.description: Multi-Layer Insulation blankets to cover the telescope barrel for thermal control.

nodes.name: MLI Blankets (SAR Electronics)
nodes.description: Multi-Layer Insulation blankets to cover the SAR electronics for thermal control.

nodes.name: Optical Solar Reflector (+X Face)
nodes.description: Optical Solar Reflector panel on +X face for passive thermal control.

nodes.name: Optical Solar Reflector (-X Face)
nodes.description: Optical Solar Reflector panel on -X face for passive thermal control.

nodes.name: Telescope Barrel
nodes.description: CFRP barrel for the TMA telescope, providing athermal performance (CTE < 1 × 10^-6 /°C).

nodes.name: TMA Primary Mirror (M1)
nodes.description: First freeform mirror of the Three-Mirror Anastigmat (TMA) telescope. Aluminum substrate with electroless nickel plating + protected gold coating. Primary aperture diameter ≥ 200 mm.

nodes.name: TMA Secondary Mirror (M2)
nodes.description: Second freeform mirror of the TMA telescope. Aluminum substrate with electroless nickel plating + protected gold coating.

nodes.name: TMA Tertiary Mirror (M3)
nodes.description: Third freeform mirror of the TMA telescope. Aluminum substrate with electroless nickel plating + protected gold coating.

nodes.name: Telescope Baffles & Light Stops
nodes.description: Internal baffling and knife-edge light stops within the telescope barrel for stray light rejection (≥ 10^-6 veiling glare index).

nodes.name: FPA Conduction Strap
nodes.description: Conduction strap for passive cooling of the Focal Plane Assembly to the -Z radiator panel.

nodes.name: AI Accelerator Aluminum Vault
nodes.description: 2 mm thick aluminum vault enclosing the Jetson Orin NX for radiation shielding.

nodes.name: AI Accelerator Tantalum Spot Shielding
nodes.description: 0.5 mm tantalum spot shielding over critical logic components of the Jetson Orin NX.

nodes.name: PC/104 Stacking Spacers
nodes.description: Standoffs for stacking PC/104 compliant boards within the CubeSat chassis.

nodes.name: M2.5 Fasteners (Internal)
nodes.description: General purpose M2.5 fasteners for internal component mounting.

nodes.name: M3 Fasteners (Chassis/Panels)
nodes.description: General purpose M3 fasteners for chassis panels and structural elements.

nodes.name: Mechanical Deployment Inhibits
nodes.description: Three mechanical inhibits to prevent unintended deployment before launch, as per CSLI safety requirements.

nodes.name: Harness Cable Ties
nodes.description: Cable ties for securing internal power and signal harnesses.

nodes.name: Harness Routing Troughs
nodes.description: Custom 3D printed troughs to separate power, signal, and RF harnesses, maintaining minimum 25mm separation.

nodes.name: 28V Unregulated Power Bus
nodes.description: Distributes unregulated 28V power to connected components.

nodes.name: 12V Regulated Power Bus
nodes.description: Distributes regulated 12V power to connected components.

nodes.name: 5V Regulated Power Bus
nodes.description: Distributes regulated 5V power to connected components.

nodes.name: 3.3V Regulated Power Bus
nodes.description: Distributes regulated 3.3V power to connected components.

originalPrompt:
================================================================================
URBANEYE — 12U CUBESAT HARDWARE BLUEPRINT PROMPT
For use with blueprint.am AI Hardware Design Tool
================================================================================

PROJECT NAME: UrbanEye
TYPE: 12U CubeSat (226 mm × 226 mm × 340 mm)
MISSION: High-resolution Earth Observation satellite for detecting illegal
construction and unregistered buildings in rapidly urbanizing cities (primary
target: Baku, Azerbaijan). Uses dual-modality sensing — panchromatic/multispectral
optical imaging at 30–50 cm GSD and X-band SAR — combined with onboard Edge AI
inference to identify construction anomalies before downlinking compact alert
packets to a ground-based GIS compliance platform.
ORBIT: Sun-Synchronous Orbit (SSO), 500–550 km altitude, LTAN 10:30 AM
DESIGN LIFE: 3 years
TOTAL DRY MASS TARGET: ≤ 19 kg (with 20% margin)
AVERAGE POWER GENERATION: ~80 W (Beginning of Life, SSO)

================================================================================
SECTION 1 — MECHANICAL STRUCTURE
================================================================================

CHASSIS:
- Material: Aluminum alloy 7075-T6 primary structure; Grade 5 Titanium
  (Ti-6Al-4V) brackets for high-stress interfaces
- Configuration: 12U CubeSat chassis with 6U upper bay (payload) and
  6U lower bay (bus subsystems)
- Standard: PC/104 and CubeSat Design Specification (CDS) Rev 14 compliant
- Payload deck: Carbon-fiber reinforced polymer (CFRP) optical bench plate
  for athermal telescope mounting; bipod flexure mounts to isolate thermal
  distortion
- Deployable mechanisms: 4× spring-loaded solar array hinge assemblies
  (must survive 14.1 g_rms random vibration); 1× SAR antenna deployment
  hinge (torsion spring, fail-safe deployed)
- Separation system: CubeSat spring ejector compatible with Nanoracks,
  Exolaunch, or D-Orbit ION deployers

MASS ALLOCATION (per subsystem):
- Optical telescope + detector assembly: 3.5 kg
- SAR antenna assembly (stowed): 2.8 kg
- OBC + AI processor boards: 0.8 kg
- ADCS unit (wheels + star trackers + IMU): 1.4 kg
- EPS (PCDU + battery pack): 1.8 kg
- Deployable solar array panels (×4): 1.2 kg
- X-Band communication module: 0.6 kg
- S-Band TT&C module: 0.3 kg
- Structure + harness + thermal hardware: 3.0 kg
- Mass storage (flash modules): 0.3 kg
- System margin (20%): 3.2 kg
TOTAL: ≤ 19.0 kg

THERMAL CONTROL:
- Passive radiator panel (−Z face, deep-space pointing) for focal plane cooling
- Kapton heater mats on battery pack (thermostatically controlled, 2 W each)
- Multi-Layer Insulation (MLI) blankets on telescope barrel and SAR electronics
- Black anodized surfaces on structural panels for passive radiation
- Optical solar reflectors (OSR) on +X and −X faces
- Target focal plane temperature: −10°C ± 2°C (operational)
- Battery temperature range: 0°C to +25°C (operational), −20°C to +40°C (survival)

================================================================================
SECTION 2 — OPTICAL PAYLOAD SUBSYSTEM
================================================================================

TELESCOPE ASSEMBLY:
- Design: Three-Mirror Anastigmat (TMA), off-axis, afocal front end
- Primary aperture diameter: ≥ 200 mm (clear aperture)
- Effective focal length (EFL): 1,200–1,600 mm
- F-number: f/6 to f/8
- Optical prescription: three freeform mirrors (M1, M2, M3) in Korsch-type
  arrangement; all mirrors are aluminum substrate with electroless nickel
  plating + protected gold coating
- Wavefront error: ≤ λ/14 RMS at 550 nm (system level)
- Stray light rejection: ≥ 10^-6 veiling glare index via baffling + knife-edge
  light stops inside telescope barrel
- Telescope barrel: CFRP with CTE < 1 × 10^-6 /°C for athermal performance

FOCAL PLANE ASSEMBLY (FPA):
- Detector: Back-side illuminated (BSI) CMOS, scientific grade
  (Sony IMX455 heritage or equivalent space-screened part)
- Pixel pitch: 5.0–7.0 μm
- Array size: ≥ 9000 × 6000 pixels (for adequate swath)
- Frame rate: ≥ 30 fps burst mode; TDI (Time Delay Integration) mode for
  push-broom imaging
- Spectral channels:
    PAN (Panchromatic): 450–700 nm
    Band 1 (Blue): 450–510 nm
    Band 2 (Green): 510–580 nm
    Band 3 (Red): 625–695 nm
    Band 4 (Near-Infrared / NIR): 750–900 nm
- Readout noise: ≤ 3 e− RMS
- Full-well capacity: ≥ 50,000 e−
- MTF at Nyquist: ≥ 0.20 (system-level requirement)
- FPA cooling: passive, via conduction strap to −Z radiator panel
- FPA interface: Camera Link HS or CoaXPress serial link to FPGA pre-processor

IMAGING PARAMETERS:
- Target GSD: 30–50 cm at 500 km altitude (panchromatic)
- Swath width: 4–6 km (cross-track)
- SNR @ nadir, full sun: ≥ 50:1 (panchromatic)
- Dynamic range: ≥ 11 bits effective

================================================================================
SECTION 3 — SAR PAYLOAD SUBSYSTEM
================================================================================

RADAR SYSTEM:
- Frequency band: X-Band, center frequency 9.6 GHz
- Waveform: Linear FM chirp (LFM), pulse compression
- Bandwidth: ≥ 500 MHz (for 0.3 m range resolution)
- Pulse width: 5–50 μs (variable)
- PRF (Pulse Repetition Frequency): 2,000–4,000 Hz
- Operating modes:
    Stripmap: 5 m resolution, 10 km swath
    Spotlight: 1–3 m resolution, 5 km × 5 km scene
- Polarization: Dual-polarization VV + VH (switchable)
- Look angle: 20–45° off-nadir (steerable via ADCS)

SAR ELECTRONICS:
- Transmitter: GaN solid-state power amplifier (SSPA)
    Peak output power: 20–40 W RF
    Duty cycle: ≤ 10% (power budget constraint)
    Instantaneous power draw: 40 W peak
- Receiver: Low-noise amplifier (LNA), noise figure ≤ 2.5 dB
- Chirp generator: Direct Digital Synthesis (DDS) waveform generator,
  Analog Devices AD9914 or equivalent
- ADC: 12-bit, ≥ 1 Gsps (raw SAR data sampling)
- Frequency reference: Temperature Compensated Crystal Oscillator (TCXO),
  stability ≤ 1 ppb
- Raw data compression: onboard FPGA performs range compression,
  2:1 block adaptive quantization before storage

SAR ANTENNA:
- Type: Deployable planar microstrip patch array
- Stowed dimensions: 340 mm × 120 mm × 15 mm (fits within 12U envelope)
- Deployed dimensions: 500 mm × 300 mm (aperture)
- Number of elements: 8 × 16 patch array (128 elements)
- Gain: ≥ 28 dBi
- Beam steering: Electronic phase shifting via 4-bit phase shifters per element
  (±30° azimuth steering without mechanical pointing)
- Deployment: Single-hinge spring mechanism; pyro-free (shape memory alloy
  or burn-wire release); deploys to locked position on first command
- RF connection: Flexible coaxial harness (phase-stable over −20°C to +60°C)

================================================================================
SECTION 4 — ON-BOARD COMPUTER (OBC) & AI PROCESSOR
================================================================================

MAIN OBC:
- Board: Xiphos Q8S or equivalent (ARM Cortex-A9 dual-core + Xilinx Kintex-7
  FPGA, space-qualified)
- CPU clock: ≥ 800 MHz
- RAM: 4 GB DDR3 ECC (error-correcting)
- Boot flash: 256 MB NOR flash (redundant, A/B partitions for OTA updates)
- OS: FreeRTOS kernel + NASA cFS (core Flight System) middleware
- Radiation tolerance: ≥ 30 krad TID; SEU-mitigated FPGA configuration
- Interfaces: SpaceWire (×4), CAN 2.0B (×2), RS-422 (×4), I²C (×8),
  SPI (×4), USB 2.0 (×2, for ground use only), LVDS camera link
- Watchdog: External hardware watchdog with autonomous reset capability

FPGA CO-PROCESSOR (on OBC):
- Device: Xilinx Kintex-7 XC7K160T (radiation-tolerant SRAM FPGA with TMR)
- Functions:
    SAR raw data range compression (real-time)
    Image tile pre-processing (demosaic, bad pixel correction, flat-field)
    LVDS/Camera Link interface management
    Data routing and DMA between payload and storage

AI ACCELERATOR:
- Primary option: NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX (16 GB) — COTS, space-modified
    Performance: 100 TOPS (INT8)
    Power: 10–15 W (inference mode), 5 W (idle)
    Interface to OBC: PCIe Gen3 x4
    Shielding: Enclosed in 2 mm aluminum vault + 0.5 mm tantalum spot
    shielding over critical logic; target ≤ 10 krad TID lifetime dose
- Backup option: Ubotica CogniSAT-XE1 (radiation-hardened, 24 TOPS,
  space-qualified) — lower performance but zero radiation risk

MASS STORAGE:
- Capacity: 512 GB to 1 TB
- Technology: 3D-Plus or Microsemi rad-hard NAND flash modules
- Interface: SPI NOR (boot) + parallel NAND (bulk storage)
- Organization: FIFO ring buffer for raw imagery; priority queue for
  AI-flagged alert packets (promoted to high-priority downlink)
- Endurance: ≥ 100,000 write cycles per block
- EDAC: Hardware SECDED (Single Error Correct, Double Error Detect)
  on all memory buses

================================================================================
SECTION 5 — ADCS (ATTITUDE DETERMINATION & CONTROL SYSTEM)
================================================================================

REQUIREMENTS:
- Pointing accuracy: ≤ 0.01° (3σ) — mandatory to prevent image smear at 30 cm GSD
- Pointing knowledge: ≤ 5 arcseconds (3σ) — from star tracker outputs
- Slew rate: ≥ 3°/s for target acquisition within a single pass
- Settling time: ≤ 10 s after slew maneuver to meet pointing accuracy

SENSORS:
- Star Trackers (×2, redundant):
    Model: Blue Canyon Technologies NST, or Sinclair Interplanetary ST-16RT2
    Update rate: ≥ 4 Hz
    Pointing knowledge: ≤ 5 arcsec (3σ)
    FOV: ≥ 10° × 10°; lost-in-space capable
    Interface: RS-422 or SpaceWire to OBC
    Mounting: On +Y and −Y panels, separated by ≥ 90° in azimuth to
    avoid simultaneous sun blinding
- IMU / Gyroscope:
    Technology: MEMS fiber optic gyro or Analog Devices ADIS16480
    Angular Random Walk (ARW): ≤ 0.003°/√hr
    Bias stability: ≤ 1°/hr
    Update rate: 100 Hz (provides high-frequency attitude during imaging)
    Interface: SPI to OBC
- Sun Sensors (×6, coarse):
    One per face; fine sun sensor on +Z for precise solar tracking
    Used for initial acquisition and safe-mode attitude hold
- Magnetometer (×2, redundant):
    Used with magnetorquers for momentum desaturation scheduling

ACTUATORS:
- Reaction Wheels (×4, pyramid configuration):
    Model: Bradford WDET10, or Microwheel 200
    Angular momentum: ≥ 30 mNm·s each
    Torque: ≥ 0.5 mNm each
    Configuration: 3 orthogonal + 1 skewed 54.7° from Z-axis (redundant)
    Speed range: ±6,000 RPM
    Interface: RS-422 or CAN to ADCS controller
- Magnetorquers (×3, one per axis):
    Dipole moment: ≥ 0.3 A·m² each
    Used for desaturation of reaction wheels and safe-mode detumbling
    Interface: PWM drive from ADCS board
- ADCS Controller Board:
    Runs Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) sensor fusion at 100 Hz
    Implements PD control law for fine pointing during imaging
    Runs detumble algorithm (B-dot controller) autonomously on boot

================================================================================
SECTION 6 — ELECTRICAL POWER SYSTEM (EPS)
================================================================================

SOLAR ARRAYS:
- Panel count: 4 deployable panels (2× body-mounted fixed + 4× deployable wings)
- Cell type: Triple-junction GaAs (XTJ-Prime or equivalent)
- Cell efficiency: ≥ 30% (AM0, 28°C)
- Total array area: ~0.45 m² (deployed)
- Average power generation: ~80 W (BOL), ~68 W (EOL, 3-year degradation)
- Panel substrate: CFRP with adhesive cell bonding; coverglass CMX (500 μm)
- Deployment: Spring-loaded hinges, single-sided; locked via shape memory
  alloy wire release (redundant commands)

BATTERY:
- Chemistry: Li-ion (18650 cells or prismatic pouch format)
- Capacity: 100–150 Wh (sized for eclipse operation + SAR burst margin)
- Nominal voltage: 7.4 V (2S configuration) or 14.8 V (4S)
- State of Charge range: 20%–90% (operational), never below 15% (FDIR cutoff)
- Supplier: GomSpace BPX, Saft MP176065, or EaglePicher LCB series
- Temperature: Monitored per cell; heater mats activate if T < 5°C

POWER CONDITIONING & DISTRIBUTION UNIT (PCDU):
- Bus architecture: Unregulated 28 V primary bus
- Converter topology: Synchronous buck regulators per load rail
    Rail 1: 12 V / 5 A — OBC, FPGA
    Rail 2: 5 V / 6 A — ADCS, sensors, comms
    Rail 3: 3.3 V / 4 A — memory, low-power peripherals
    Rail 4: 28 V pass-through / 5 A — SAR PA, X-Band TX (switched)
    Rail 5: 28 V pass-through / 3 A — Jetson AI module (switched)
- Solar MPPT: Per-panel Maximum Power Point Tracking
- Supplier: GomSpace NanoPower P60 (proven 12U heritage) or
  DHV Technology EPS-R
- Overcurrent protection: eFuse per branch; latching relay on SAR rail
  (requires explicit command to re-enable after cutoff)
- Battery charging: 1C max charge rate; 0.1C trickle maintenance

POWER BUDGET SUMMARY:
Mode             | Peak (W) | Average (W) | Duty Cycle
-----------------|----------|-------------|------------
Optical imaging  |    12    |      3      | ~15% of orbit
SAR transmission |    40    |      4      | ~8% of orbit
AI inference     |    15    |      5      | ~20% of orbit
OBC + FPGA       |     8    |      6      | 100%
ADCS             |    10    |      8      | 100%
X-Band TX        |    15    |      2      | ~8% (downlink passes)
S-Band TT&C      |     4    |      2      | ~15%
Thermal heaters  |     8    |      4      | ~30% (eclipse)
EPS losses (~10%)|    —     |      3      | —
TOTAL            |   ~78 W  |    ~37 W    | —

CONSTRAINT: SAR TX and X-Band TX must NEVER operate simultaneously.
Combined peak would exceed solar generation. FSW enforces as a hard lock.

================================================================================
SECTION 7 — COMMUNICATIONS SUBSYSTEM
================================================================================

X-BAND HIGH-RATE DOWNLINK (Science Data):
- Purpose: Downlink imagery and AI alert packets
- Frequency: 8.0–8.4 GHz (X-band, ITU downlink allocation)
- Data rate: 150 Mbps (QPSK modulation, rate 7/8 turbo code)
- RF output power: 5 W (37 dBm)
- Transmitter: Syrlinks EWC27, or Tethers Unlimited SWIFT-XTS
- Antenna: 4-element patch phased array (fixed nadir-pointing)
    Gain: ~10 dBi
    Half-power beamwidth: ±25° (covers ground station at elevation > 5°)
- Link margin: ≥ 3 dB at 5° elevation angle to 3.7 m ground station
- Encryption: AES-256 on all science data downlinks
- Interface to OBC: Gigabit Ethernet or PCIe

S-BAND TT&C (Housekeeping & Commands):
- Purpose: Telemetry, Tracking & Commanding (always-on)
- Uplink frequency: 2.025–2.110 GHz
- Downlink frequency: 2.200–2.290 GHz
- Data rates: 9.6 kbps uplink (commands); 256 kbps downlink (housekeeping TLM)
- Transmitter power: 1 W RF
- Antenna: 2× patch antennas (dipole-like pattern, one on +Z, one on −Z)
  for full-sphere coverage including tumbling modes
- Protocol: CCSDS TC/TM Space Packet Protocol
- Modulation: PCM/PM/NRZ-L
- Interface to OBC: RS-422 UART

GROUND STATION:
- Primary: 3.7 m X/S-band parabolic dish (Baku or Nakhchivan)
- Backup: Commercial network (AWS Ground Station, Kongsberg SatServices)
- Expected pass duration: 5–10 minutes per pass; 2–4 passes per day
  over Baku at 525 km SSO
- Data volume per pass (X-band, 150 Mbps, 7-min contact): ~7 GB raw
- Prioritized downlink: AI alert packets (~50–200 kB each) transmitted first
  within any contact window

================================================================================
SECTION 8 — WIRING & ELECTRICAL INTERFACES
================================================================================

INTERNAL BUS ARCHITECTURE:
- SpaceWire (ECSS-E-ST-50-12C): Primary high-speed data bus
    OBC ↔ FPGA co-processor: SpaceWire × 2 (redundant)
    OBC ↔ Mass storage: SpaceWire × 2
    OBC ↔ X-Band TX: SpaceWire or GbE
- CAN 2.0B (1 Mbps): Secondary housekeeping bus
    OBC ↔ EPS/PCDU
    OBC ↔ ADCS controller
    OBC ↔ S-Band radio
- I²C (400 kHz): Sensor bus
    OBC ↔ Temperature sensors (×12 nodes)
    OBC ↔ Sun sensors (×6)
    OBC ↔ Magnetometers (×2)
- RS-422 (115200 baud): Low-speed interfaces
    OBC ↔ Star trackers (×2)
    OBC ↔ Reaction wheels (×4)
    OBC ↔ Magnetorquers (×3)
- LVDS Camera Link HS:
    FPA detector ↔ FPGA (raw image data, ~3 Gbps)
- PCIe Gen3 x4:
    OBC ↔ Jetson AI accelerator

POWER HARNESS:
- Primary 28 V bus: AWG 22 twisted shielded pair, PTFE insulation
- Secondary regulated rails: AWG 26 for signal-level loads
- Ground plane: Star-topology grounding to chassis ground point
- EMI shielding: Braided shield on all harnesses; individual shields
  grounded at source end only (single-point ground rule)
- Connector standard: Omnetics nano-D or Glenair MIL-DTL-38999 Series III
- Harness routing: Separate power and signal harness troughs;
  minimum 25 mm separation between RF and digital harnesses

PYROTECHNIC / DEPLOYMENT CIRCUITS:
- SAR antenna and solar array deployment: Shape Memory Alloy (SMA) actuators
  (pyro-free, no ITAR constraints)
- Firing circuits: Isolated, dual-redundant command paths; arm+fire sequence
  required (single-fault tolerant)
- Inhibits: 3× mechanical inhibits before launch (per CSLI safety requirements)

================================================================================
SECTION 9 — FLIGHT SOFTWARE (FSW) ARCHITECTURE
================================================================================

OS & MIDDLEWARE:
- Kernel: FreeRTOS (v10.x) on ARM Cortex-A9
- Middleware: NASA cFS (core Flight System) — app-based modular architecture
- Scheduling: Rate-monotonic scheduling; critical ADCS loop at 100 Hz,
  housekeeping at 1 Hz, AI inference task at 0.1 Hz (10 s cadence)

KEY cFS APPLICATIONS:
1. sch_lab: Scheduler — triggers all app execution on timer ticks
2. ci_lab / to_lab: Command Ingest and Telemetry Output apps
3. fm: File Manager — manages flash storage, image file FIFO
4. cs: Checksum app — memory integrity checking
5. lc: Limit Checker — FDIR rule evaluation
6. sc: Stored Command — autonomous sequence execution from uploaded scripts
7. ds: Data Storage — telemetry archival
8. HS: Health & Safety app — watchdog, CPU utilization monitoring
9. ADCS_APP: Custom — runs EKF sensor fusion + control law at 100 Hz
10. PAYLOAD_APP: Custom — triggers FPA exposures, manages image buffers
11. SAR_APP: Custom — manages SAR waveform timing, pulse scheduling
12. AI_INFER_APP: Custom — loads TensorRT model on Jetson, runs inference,
    generates alert telemetry packets
13. COMMS_APP: Custom — manages downlink queues, contact window scheduling,
    CCSDS framing

AUTONOMY & FDIR RULES:
- Battery SOC < 40%: Suspend payload operations; enter Power Safe mode
- Battery SOC < 20%: Enter Emergency Safe mode; only TT&C active
- Focal plane temp > −5°C: Suspend imaging; increase radiator exposure
- SAR duty cycle > 12% in 10-min window: Auto-inhibit SAR until window resets
- Wheel saturation (any wheel > 80% momentum): Schedule desaturation pass
- OBC watchdog timeout (1 s): Cold reset OBC; attempt recovery from last safe config
- Single-event upset on critical register (detected by TMR): Log + correct; if
  repeated 3× in 60 s, reboot subsystem

================================================================================
SECTION 10 — AI/ML ONBOARD PIPELINE
================================================================================

MODEL ARCHITECTURE:
- Change Detection Head: Siamese U-Net
    Input: Dual-channel (current pass + baseline reference), 256×256 px tiles
    Encoder: ResNet-34 backbone (shared weights, pretrained on ImageNet)
    Output: Binary change mask + anomaly probability per pixel
    Parameters: ~28M (FP32), ~7M effective (INT8 quantized)
- SAR Coherence Module: Lightweight CNN
    Input: SAR complex coherence patch (magnitude + phase)
    Output: New-structure probability map
    Parameters: ~2M (INT8)
- Fusion Head: Vision Transformer (ViT-Tiny variant)
    Input: Concatenated optical change mask + SAR coherence map
    Output: Per-parcel anomaly score [0.0 – 1.0]
    Parameters: ~8M (INT8)
    Inference time target: < 2 seconds per 256×256 tile on Jetson Orin NX

TRAINING DATA:
- Optical: WorldView-2/3 pan-sharpened imagery over Baku + 5 comparable cities;
  manually labeled change polygons from cadastral + aerial inspection records
- SAR: Sentinel-1 GRD + SLC data over same AOIs; coherence computed via SNAP
- Synthetic augmentation: Random rotation, radiometric jitter, cloud simulation,
  seasonal variation augmentation to improve robustness

INFERENCE PIPELINE (per imaging pass):
Step 1 — FPGA Pre-processing: Radiometric calibration (dark frame, flat field,
         bad pixel map); orthorectification using onboard ephemeris + ADCS
         quaternions; tile into 256×256 px patches with 32 px overlap.
Step 2 — Optical Feature Extraction: Run Siamese U-Net on each tile pair
         (current vs. baseline). Generate change probability map.
Step 3 — SAR Coherence Analysis: Compute interferometric coherence between
         current and previous SAR acquisitions. Run SAR CNN.
Step 4 — Fusion Inference: Merge optical + SAR outputs through ViT fusion
         head. Compute per-parcel anomaly score.
Step 5 — Confidence Gate: Suppress alerts where anomaly score < 0.75.
         Apply morphological filtering (remove isolated pixels < 5 m²).
Step 6 — Alert Packet Generation: For each passing detection, encode:
         {GPS centroid, bounding polygon GeoJSON, anomaly score,
          acquisition UTC timestamp, thumbnail JPEG2000 64×64 px,
          model confidence, optical+SAR band flags}.
         Total packet size: 50–200 kB per detection.
         Promote all alert packets to Priority-1 downlink queue.

EXPECTED PERFORMANCE (target):
- Building change detection F1 score: > 0.82
- False alarm rate: < 5% (with 0.75 confidence threshold)
- Average downlink data reduction vs. raw imagery: ~97%

================================================================================
SECTION 11 — GROUND SEGMENT & GIS PLATFORM
================================================================================

DATA RECEPTION:
- L0 depacketization: CCSDS TM frame sync → extract science packets → reconstruct
  image files; pipeline running on AWS EC2 (c5.4xlarge) or GCP Compute
- L1A: Radiometric calibration (absolute + relative); metadata tagging
- L1B: Orthorectification + georeferencing (RPC model from onboard ephemeris)
- L2: Atmospheric correction (6S radiative transfer model); reflectance product
- Archive: Cloud-native GeoTIFF COG (Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF) stored in S3/GCS;
  queryable via SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) API

GIS COMPLIANCE PLATFORM:
- Database: PostGIS (PostgreSQL + spatial extension); stores alert polygons,
  parcel boundaries, permit records
- Cadastral integration: API connection to State Committee on Property Issues
  (Azerbaijan) for real-time permit status lookup
- Alert workflow: Detected change polygon → spatial join with cadastral DB →
  if no valid permit found → generate compliance alert → assign to inspector
- Dashboard: React web application + QGIS plugin for field inspectors;
  mobile-responsive; shows imagery chip + SAR coherence overlay + parcel info
- Notification: Webhook → inspectors receive push notification within 2 hours
  of satellite pass over their jurisdiction
- Legal audit trail: SHA-256 hash of each alert packet stored on permissioned
  blockchain (Hyperledger Fabric) for legal evidentiary chain of custody

================================================================================
SECTION 12 — INTEGRATION & TEST PLAN SUMMARY
================================================================================

PHASE A (Months 1–6): Concept & Requirements
- Mission requirements document; orbital analysis; trade studies
- ITU frequency coordination filing for X-band SAR (BEGIN IMMEDIATELY)
- Component long-lead procurement list; ITAR/EAR export license applications

PHASE B (Months 6–14): Preliminary Design + FlatSat
- FlatSat: All engineering model boards laid flat on bench; validate all
  electrical interfaces: SpaceWire, CAN, I²C, LVDS, RS-422, power rails
- Telescope breadboard: Measure MTF, distortion map, stray light rejection
- SAR bench test: Validate GaN PA output power, antenna element patterns,
  radar equation closure at 500 km range
- AI model training complete; F1 > 0.82 validated on test dataset
- Preliminary Design Review (PDR)

PHASE C (Months 14–26): Detailed Design + Manufacturing
- PCBs fabricated to IPC-6012 Class 3; conformal coated
- CFRP optical bench machined and characterised for CTE
- Deployable solar array and SAR antenna mechanisms fabricated; 3× deploy cycle test
- FSW integration and Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testing
- AI model quantized to INT8 TensorRT; profiled on Jetson: < 2 s per tile confirmed
- Critical Design Review (CDR)

PHASE D (Months 26–36): AIT & Environmental Qualification
- TVAC: −20°C to +60°C, 8 thermal cycles, 10^-5 torr vacuum
- Random Vibration: 14.1 g_rms per Falcon 9 Transporter rideshare spec
- Sine Sweep: 5–100 Hz per launch vehicle user guide
- Shock: 1,000 g, 0.5 ms half-sine (deployment shock)
- EMC/EMI: MIL-STD-461G; verify SAR TX does not interfere with receiver/OBC
- End-to-End Functional Test: Full imaging pass simulation in cleanroom
- Pre-Ship Review (PSR)

PHASE E (Month 36+): Launch & Operations
- Launch vehicle: SpaceX Transporter rideshare, Falcon 9 ESPA configuration
- Deployer: Exolaunch EXOpod or D-Orbit ION carrier
- LEOP (Days 1–14): Beacon acquisition → solar array deploy → 3-axis control
- Commissioning (Weeks 2–8): First-light imaging, geometric calibration,
  AI model validation against Baku ground truth
- Nominal Ops: Automated daily imaging schedule; 2–4 city passes/day;
  alert packets delivered to GIS platform within 2 hours of imaging

================================================================================
END OF URBANEYE BLUEPRINT PROMPT
Revision: 1.0 | Date: April 2026 | Classification: Engineering Working Document
================================================================================
projectDescription: The UrbanEye Satellite is a 12U CubeSat for high-resolution Earth observation, featuring a 7075-T6 aluminum chassis, CFRP optical bench, and dual optical/X-band SAR payloads. Onboard AI, powered by a Jetson Orin NX, detects construction anomalies, with data downlinked via X-band to a ground GIS platform.
